task_pools.display_name: MVP QC Laboratory Equipment Qualification and Calibration Linkage Task Pool
source_limitations: Wave 2 expands task-pool source content only.
Duration references are provisional and require Wave 3 planning-basis coverage.
Document expectations require Wave 4 template bodies and Wave 5 schema/DCF coverage before product-ready output.
Task-pool source records do not authorize productization, deployment, SaaS readiness, customer-ready release, or M29.12 UAT acceptance.

tasks.title: Capture QC lab equipment change and scope context
tasks.purpose: Capture change/control context, instrument identity, lab use, GMP data role, calibration linkage, lifecycle route, and qualification boundary.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route

document_expectations.notes: Wave 2 validation fix: qualification plan expectation is explicitly represented at task-pool level; template/body/schema implementation remains Wave 4/Wave 5.

notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Confirm calibration and service status
tasks.purpose: Verify calibration status, calibration procedure availability, service readiness, and reference standard needs before qualification use.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Confirm lab equipment requirements and vendor document basis
tasks.purpose: Capture intended use, method use, URS/requirements basis, vendor documents, and instrument specification inputs for RTM.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Perform lab equipment RA/FMEA and CSV linkage assessment
tasks.purpose: Assess GMP impact, data integrity role, calibration impact, CSV/software linkage, and testing depth.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Establish lab equipment RTM basis
tasks.purpose: Map URS/vendor/specification requirements to IQ/OQ/PQ or verification evidence.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Perform lab equipment DQ or design/specification review
tasks.purpose: Confirm design/specification suitability, vendor evidence, calibration requirements, and readiness for qualification.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Prepare and execute lab equipment IQ/OQ package
tasks.purpose: Verify installation and operational performance for the defined instrument context.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Prepare and execute lab equipment PQ/performance verification where applicable
tasks.purpose: Verify method/use performance or periodic verification where PQ/performance evidence is in scope.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

tasks.title: Close QC lab equipment qualification package
tasks.purpose: Confirm qualification evidence, calibration linkage, CSV linkage outcome, deviations/CAPA, and VSR readiness.
prerequisites: Human-confirmed scope and applicable lifecycle route
notes: Wave 2 task-pool expansion source record; not a document generator.

library_controls: Wave 2 expands task-pool source records only.
Document expectations remain controlled references for later template/schema waves.
Duration references are provisional until Wave 3 planning-basis expansion resolves them.
This library does not generate documents, approve records, release outputs, or accept M29.12 UAT.
This library does not authorize productization, deployment, SaaS readiness, customer-ready release, or M29.12 UAT acceptance.
